explain.rule: Reported speech reports what someone said without quoting exactly. When reporting verb (said, told) is past, tense shifts back: present simple → past simple, present continuous → past continuous, past simple → past perfect, will → would, can → could, must → had to, may → might. Pronouns and time words also change: I → he/she, here → there, now → then, today → that day, tomorrow → the next day. Say + that clause. Tell + person + that clause.
explain.why_matters: Essential for retelling conversations, reporting news, and relaying information. Appears constantly in speech and writing.
explain.hebrew_note: Hebrew often keeps the original tense: 'הוא אמר שהוא עייף' (he said he IS tired). English requires backshift: 'He said he WAS tired.' Also: say vs tell. Say = say + clause. Tell = tell + person + clause. 'He told that' = WRONG. 'He said me' = WRONG.
explain.tip: Say vs tell: SAY + (that) clause — no person. TELL + person + clause. No tense backshift needed for general truths: 'She said the Earth is round.' Reported questions use normal word order (no inversion): 'She asked where I lived' NOT 'She asked where did I live'.

good_examples.sentence: She said she was feeling much better.
good_examples.audio_text: She said she was feeling much better.
good_examples.note: 'I am feeling better' → was feeling (tense shift)

good_examples.sentence: He told me he had already finished the report.
good_examples.audio_text: He told me he had already finished the report.
good_examples.note: 'I have finished' → had finished (present perfect → past perfect)

good_examples.sentence: They said they would call later.
good_examples.audio_text: They said they would call later.

good_examples.sentence: She asked if I had seen the film.
good_examples.audio_text: She asked if I had seen the film.
good_examples.note: Yes/no question in reported form uses 'if'

good_examples.sentence: He wanted to know where she lived.
good_examples.audio_text: He wanted to know where she lived.
good_examples.note: Wh-question — normal word order, no inversion

good_examples.sentence: She told me not to worry.
good_examples.audio_text: She told me not to worry.
good_examples.note: Reported negative command: told + person + not to + verb

good_examples.sentence: He said he couldn't come to the meeting.
good_examples.audio_text: He said he couldn't come to the meeting.

good_examples.sentence: They told us the results would be ready the following day.
good_examples.audio_text: They told us the results would be ready the following day.

good_examples.sentence: He explained that water boils at 100 degrees.
good_examples.audio_text: He explained that water boils at 100 degrees.
good_examples.note: General truth — no tense change needed

good_examples.sentence: She said she had been waiting for over an hour.
good_examples.audio_text: She said she had been waiting for over an hour.

bad_examples.wrong: He said he is tired. (past conversation)
bad_examples.reason: When reporting verb is past (said), tense shifts back.

bad_examples.wrong: She told that she was coming.
bad_examples.correct: She said that she was coming. / She told me that she was coming.
bad_examples.reason: Tell must be followed by a person. Say does NOT take a person.

bad_examples.wrong: He said me he was busy.
bad_examples.correct: He told me he was busy.
bad_examples.reason: Say does not take a direct person. Told + person.

bad_examples.wrong: She asked where did he live.
bad_examples.reason: Reported questions do NOT invert. Normal word order.

bad_examples.wrong: He said he will call. (past conversation)

exercises.question: Direct: 'I am tired.' → Reported: She said she ___ tired.
Direct: 'I have finished.' → He said he ___ finished.
Direct: 'I will call.' → She said she ___ call.
Direct: 'Can you help?' → She asked if I ___ help her.
She ___ (tell) me she was going to be late.
exercises.explanation: told me — tell takes a person.

exercises.question: He said he ___ (not/can) come to the party.
She asked where I ___ (live).
exercises.explanation: lived — no inversion, tense shifts back.

exercises.question: They told us the results ___ (be) ready the next day.
He said he ___ (study) when she called.
She asked me ___ I ___ (see) the new film.
He told me ___ (not/be) late.
exercises.hint: Reported negative command: not to + verb

exercises.question: She said she ___ (may/be) late.
He said he ___ working on the project.
She told ___ she was feeling better.
He asked ___ I had finished.
She asked where ___ from.
exercises.hint: Normal word order in reported question
exercises.distractors: did I come / I did come / came I

exercises.question: They said the meeting ___ at 9am.
Which correctly reports: 'I must leave now.'?
exercises.answer: She said she had to leave.
exercises.explanation: must → had to (must has no past form).
exercises.distractors: She said she must leave. / She said she musted leave. / She said she should leave.

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'He said he is coming.' (past conversation)
exercises.hint: Tense must shift when reporting verb is past

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'She told that she was busy.'
exercises.answer: She said that she was busy. / She told me that she was busy.
exercises.hint: tell requires a person; say does not

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'He said me he was hungry.'
exercises.answer: He told me he was hungry.
exercises.hint: say does not take a person

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'She asked where did he work.'
exercises.explanation: where he worked — normal word order.

exercises.question: Put the words in the correct order:
exercises.answer: She said she was too tired to come.

exercises.question: Put the words in the correct order:
exercises.answer: He told me he had already finished.

exercises.question: Put the words in the correct order:
exercises.hint: Normal word order in reported question
exercises.explanation: asked where I lived — no inversion.

speak.sentence: She told me she had been waiting for over an hour and was really frustrated.
chunks: She told me she had been waiting for over an hour

speak.sentence: He said he would call me back but I still haven't heard from him.
chunks: He said he would call me back
but I still haven't heard from him.

speak.sentence: She asked me if I had ever been to Japan and whether I would recommend it.
chunks: She asked me if I had ever been to Japan
and whether I would recommend it.

speak.sentence: He told me not to worry about the deadline and that he would handle it.
chunks: He told me not to worry about the deadline
and that he would handle it.

speak.sentence: They announced that the results would be published the following week.
chunks: would be published the following week.

speak.sentence: She said she couldn't understand why the project had been cancelled.
chunks: why the project had been cancelled.

speak.sentence: He asked where I was from and how long I had been living here.
chunks: He asked where I was from
and how long I had been living here.
speak.phonetic_hints: Normal word order in reported questions.

speak.sentence: The manager explained that the decision had already been made by the board.
chunks: The manager explained that the decision
had already been made by the board.
